name: Snails, Sicilian Style
recipeInstructions:
Serves 6 to 8.
1. Use a toothpick to remove the operculum from each snail; it will come off easily.
2. Rinse the snails thoroughly in cold water, drain, and rinse again with salted cold water.
3. Add about three tablespoons of salt and bring four quarts of cold water to a boil in a kettle. Place the snails in the boiling water and simmer for six minutes, stirring occasionally.
4. In a small bowl, crush garlic and add one teaspoon of salt. Mix in olive oil, freshly ground black pepper, lemon juice, and six tablespoons cold water. Whisk until blended.
5. Drain the cooked snails and serve hot with the sauce served chilled.
Note: Remove snail meat with a pick and dip each piece into the sauce. The entire snail is edible, though some prefer to avoid the soft digestive tract at the tail end.
